text.title: Pool lifecycle phases
text.summary: Four phases drive the pool from cold start to terminal drain. Phases 1 and 4 are one-shot bookends; phases 2 and 3 form the steady-state loop, where 3a (cron tick) and 3b (completion handler) run continuously until the queue empties.

section.title: Phase 1 — Initialize state
section.p: One-time setup before any subagent is dispatched.

list.item: Build `QUEUE.txt`: one absolute target path (or batch slug) per line, ordered by intended dispatch order.
Create empty `DONE.txt`. Each completed batch appends a last-line marker `done=<slug> commit=<sha>`.
Optional `ACTIVE.txt` records currently in-flight batch slugs, one per line; it lets a recovering parent reconstruct the pool after session restart without re-reading `git log`.
Write and commit `BRIEF.md` (the single source of truth handed to each subagent: read-files, work skeleton, commit style, merge policy).
`.gitignore` `QUEUE.txt`, `DONE.txt`, `ACTIVE.txt`, and any `.claude/worktrees/` path used for isolation.
Schedule the cron tick (e.g. `/loop 5m <tick-prompt>`). The tick prompt is the verbatim instruction block the parent re-enters every interval.

section.title: Phase 2 — Initial dispatch
section.p: Fill the pool to N in one burst. Reads the first N batches off `QUEUE.txt`, pops them atomically, and dispatches one background `Agent` per batch in worktree isolation. The parent emits one summary line for the user (`Pool=N: K batches dispatched. Queue=M done=0.`) and then yields.

The pool size N is bounded by quota: read `/tmp/claude-session-state.json` `rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage` and stop adding agents when projected wave consumption would push the meter past the user-set threshold.

section.title: Phase 3 — Steady-state loop
section.p: The pool stays full through two complementary replenishment signals. Either signal alone is insufficient; both together are resilient.

section.title: 3a — Cron tick (every 5 min)

list.item: Count active background agents (via system reminders or by reading `ACTIVE.txt`).
If `active < N` AND `QUEUE.txt` non-empty, pop `(N - active) * batch_size` lines and dispatch enough replacements to refill the pool.
`git push origin main` and emit one summary line: `Tick: pool=N queue=M done=K`.

section.p: The tick is the keepalive — it covers the case where the parent is idle, lost a completion notification, or has been compacted. It also detects drain (queue empty AND zero active) and triggers terminal teardown.

section.title: 3b — Completion handler (per task-notification)

list.item: Append `done=<slug> commit=<sha>` to `DONE.txt`; remove the slug from `ACTIVE.txt` if maintained.
Pop next `batch_size` lines off `QUEUE.txt` and dispatch one replacement.
Emit one summary line: `Pool=N: <prev-id> done, replacement <new-id> dispatched. Queue=M done=K`.

section.p: The completion handler is the real-time replacement — it keeps the pool full between cron ticks. Strictly one-per-completion (not batched) once the initial pool has been seeded, so back-pressure stays smooth instead of bursty.

section.title: Phase 4 — Drain and terminate
section.p: When `QUEUE.txt` is empty AND no active agents remain, the parent runs the terminal sequence.

list.item: `git push origin main` to publish the final merges.
Emit one final summary: `Done: K batches × <batch_size> paths = N enriched. M skipped.`

If termination was triggered by quota exhaustion (3+ "out of extra usage" failures), restore the failed batches to the head of `QUEUE.txt`, delete the cron, and alert the user with the resume-time hint from the error.
